<findings>

[suggestion] [info] Zone "sources" has files across 8 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
[pattern] [warning] AppEnvironment uses a static shared singleton (line 14) as its access point. This means the composition root is globally reachable from any file that imports the module, not just from the app entry point — the intended constraint (only AppEnvironment decides when and how the model changes) is unenforced by the type system.
[relationship] [info] The bidirectional coupling between 'sources' and 'sources-gotobed' is a composition-root artifact: AppEnvironment (sources) imports SettingsWindowController and other UI components (sources-gotobed) while those components import GoToBedCore models. Extracting the composition wiring into a dedicated AppDelegate or SceneDelegate file would make the coupling directional and eliminate the zone-level bidirectionality.

</findings>

<insights>

- Zone "sources" has files across 8 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- AppEnvironment.swift (in this zone) is explicitly documented as the composition root — it wires Store → SchedulerEngine → OverlayController in its initializer and uses a closure to deliver Schedule from scheduler.onFire to overlay.present(). This single file is the origin of the bidirectional coupling between 'sources' ↔ 'sources-gotobed' and 'sources' ↔ 'log-overlaycontroller': AppEnvironment imports both execution-tier services and UI components while those components import domain models back through GoToBedCore.
- The store field on AppEnvironment is declared public (line 16), directly exposing the mutable Store to any consumer that holds an AppEnvironment reference. Settings-ui views that go through AppEnvironment coordinator methods are correctly shielded, but any code that accesses env.store directly bypasses the write-proxy contract described in AppEnvironment's own documentation.
- AppEnvironment uses a static shared singleton (line 14) as its access point. This means the composition root is globally reachable from any file that imports the module, not just from the app entry point — the intended constraint (only AppEnvironment decides when and how the model changes) is unenforced by the type system.
- The bidirectional coupling between 'sources' and 'sources-gotobed' is a composition-root artifact: AppEnvironment (sources) imports SettingsWindowController and other UI components (sources-gotobed) while those components import GoToBedCore models. Extracting the composition wiring into a dedicated AppDelegate or SceneDelegate file would make the coupling directional and eliminate the zone-level bidirectionality.

</insights>
